# Live AI Stylist — Real-Time Outfit Advice on Your Phone | LiveStylist
URL: https://livestylist.app/

Live AI vision + voice

# LiveStylist your live AI style bestie

Live styling sessions in your pocket

Talk to a live AI personal stylist. Point your phone camera, get real-time spoken outfit and beauty advice — five-minute sessions, personalised to you, ready whenever you are.

Coming soon Coming soon

## What you get

Everything you need for real-time, personalised style advice.

### See you, know you

AI vision analyses your hair, makeup and outfit in real time. It sees what you see in the mirror — and offers advice in the moment.

### Talk naturally

Have a real voice chat with your AI stylist. Interrupt, ask follow-ups, get fresh ideas — just like texting a friend who actually knows fashion.

### Style memory

Remembers your favourite colours, occasions and past sessions. Every chat builds on the last so the advice gets sharper over time.

## How it works

Three steps. Five minutes. Real advice.

1

### Start a session

Open the app, pick the vibe — date night, work, casual — and you're in.

2

### Show your look

Point the selfie camera at yourself. The AI sees your hair, makeup and outfit in real time.

3

### Get advice

Hear personalised tips, ask follow-ups, see preview images — all in one conversation.

## Pick your plan

Start free. Upgrade when you're ready.

## Questions?

The short answers. Privacy Policy & Terms have the full ones.

What is LiveStylist?

LiveStylist is your AI style bestie. Using your camera and voice, it analyses your look in real time and gives personalised tips — like having a knowledgeable friend who always knows what looks good.

How does the AI vision work?

The selfie camera streams frames to our backend, where multiple vision agents look at your eyes, mouth and outfit. The results are sent to a voice agent that talks you through advice in real time. Frames are processed and discarded — nothing is stored permanently.

Is LiveStylist free?

You get one free 5-minute session at signup, no card required. After that, Premium is $14.99/month or $119.99/year (~33% off) for 30 sessions a month, style memory and session history.

What can it help me with?

Outfit picks, makeup tips, hair ideas, colour coordination, occasion dressing (date, office, casual, weekend), and building a personal style that gets sharper the more you use it.

Is my camera data private?

Yes. Camera frames and voice audio are processed in real time and not permanently stored. Everything in transit uses TLS. Generated preview images auto-delete after 24 hours. See our Privacy Policy for the full story.

What devices is it available on?

iPhone and Android — coming soon to the App Store and Google Play.

## More from this builder

Indie tools by the same team. Same focus on speed, privacy and EU sovereignty.

### Posta

Generate, schedule and publish social content across 9 platforms — Instagram, TikTok, X, LinkedIn and more.

Visit getposta.app →

### Eurobase

EU-sovereign Backend-as-a-Service. GDPR-native alternative to Firebase and Supabase. Powers LiveStylist's backend.

Visit eurobase.app →

### Stupid Correlations

Funny correlations from completely unrelated real data — a viral-content generator for the curious and the chaotic.

Visit stupidcorrelations.app →

## Ready for your style sesh?

Your AI stylist is one tap away. Download the app and start your first session.

# About LiveStylist — AI Personal Stylist App
URL: https://livestylist.app/about.html
LastModified: 2026-06-20

# About LiveStylist

Indie app · made in the UK · backend in the EU

LiveStylist is an AI personal stylist that lives on your phone. You open the app, pick the occasion — date night, work, casual, weekend — point the selfie camera at yourself, and a five-minute voice conversation begins. The AI sees what you're wearing, how your hair is sitting, what makeup you've got on, and offers personalised tips in real time. Ask follow-ups, get alternatives, see preview images of suggested looks. Then it ends. Five minutes, real advice, no doomscroll.

## What we're building

Most "AI fashion" apps today are static: upload a photo, get a generated outfit board, share it. We wanted something closer to the experience of having a knowledgeable friend in the room while you're getting dressed — live, conversational, occasion-aware, and short enough to fit into the actual moment you're trying to decide what to wear.

The result is a focused, time-boxed live session. Multi-agent vision (eye / mouth / outfit) keeps the analysis accurate; a voice agent handles the conversation; preview images render alongside so you can see suggestions, not just hear them.

## Who's behind it

LiveStylist is built by an indie team led by Stefan Gimeson, an independent developer who also builds Posta (social content scheduling), Eurobase (EU-sovereign Backend-as-a-Service), and Stupid Correlations (viral-content generator). Same focus across all four: small, fast, privacy-respecting tools that do one thing well.

## Where the backend lives

LiveStylist runs on Eurobase — an EU-sovereign Backend-as-a-Service hosted in France (Scaleway). All profile and session data is stored on EU infrastructure, with zero exposure to the US CLOUD Act. The AI vision and voice layers (Google Gemini Live) are stateless: camera frames and audio are processed in real time and discarded, never persisted. Style preview images render via Fal.ai and auto-delete after 24 hours.

## Our stance on data

We collect the minimum possible. There's no email, no password, no real-name requirement — your account is an anonymous device ID. You can delete everything with one tap in the app. The full story is in our Privacy Policy; the practical version is: we built LiveStylist to give style advice, not to harvest identity.

## Try it

LiveStylist is launching mid-2026 on iOS and Android. The free trial gives you one full 5-minute session with no card required. Curious about specific occasions? See how it handles different occasions, or go straight to the download page.

← Back to home

# Use cases — LiveStylist
URL: https://livestylist.app/use-cases/
LastModified: 2026-06-20

Home → Use cases

# Occasions LiveStylist handles

Each occasion has its own logic — colour, layering, how the face reads, what works close up. The AI calibrates to the moment.

### Date Night

Hair, makeup, neckline, accessories — how the top half reads under restaurant lighting, up close, for hours.

Read more →

### Work

Shirt, blazer, knit, collar, tidy hair — repeatable confidence for meetings, presentations and video calls.

Read more →

### Casual

Layered tops, undone hair, light makeup, one hero piece — effortless without looking sloppy. Harder than it looks.

Read more →

### Weekend

Brunch, drinks, going out — looks that adapt across the day without you needing to rethink the top half.

Read more →

### First date

Honest, not safe. One statement, rest quiet. The five minutes before you walk out the door.

Read more →

Five-minute sessions, real personalised advice, on iOS and Android. Download the app →

# AI outfit advice for date night — LiveStylist
URL: https://livestylist.app/use-cases/date-night.html
LastModified: 2026-06-20

Use cases → Date Night

# AI outfit advice for date night

Date night styling is its own thing. The room is usually dimmer than you think, you'll be talking up close for hours, and the small things — hair, neckline, what your lip does under candlelight — carry more weight than they would in daylight. LiveStylist's date-night sessions optimise for exactly this: what flatters under restaurant lighting, how the hair and makeup land alongside the top half of your look, what reads confident across the evening.

## What the AI looks at

Face framing — how hair and neckline interact, what's drawing the eye where you want it

Colour against your skin tone in warm vs. cool light

Hair styling that holds up across the evening (humidity, sitting for hours)

Makeup intensity — soft glam vs. statement vs. understated for the light you'll actually be in

Accessories that work close-up — earrings, a necklace, glasses — and what to leave off

## Example tips you might hear

The AI talks like a friend who knows their stuff — direct, specific, not preachy. A few illustrative lines from sessions:

"That neckline is great — pair it with a delicate chain, skip the chunky pendant."

"Your hair will collapse by dinner. Pin a section back at the crown — gives it height without obvious styling."

"Mid-tone lip, soft eye — competing with both reads heavy under candlelight."

"Push the sleeves up. Defines the forearm, makes the whole top read more deliberate."

## How a session works for date night

Open the LiveStylist app, pick Date Night from the occasion picker, and tap Start. You have five minutes. The AI opens with a quick read on what you're wearing, then asks if there's anything specific you want to focus on (a piece you're unsure about, the venue, the vibe). From there it's a conversation — you adjust, it re-evaluates, you ask follow-ups. Preview images of suggested looks render alongside the voice. At minute five the session wraps with a short summary you can save to your style memory.

## Common questions

How long is a date-night session?

Five minutes. Long enough for the AI to see your look, give meaningful advice and answer follow-ups, short enough that you can do it while you're getting ready without it eating your whole evening.

Can it tell me what to wear on a first date?

Yes — open the session, tell the AI it's a first date, mention the venue if you know it. The AI tailors advice to vibe (relaxed vs. formal vs. somewhere in between) and to how visible you want to be.

What if I want a second opinion mid-conversation?

Ask. "Should I add a bolder lip?" or "What if I tied my hair up?" — the AI re-evaluates against what it's seeing and answers in plain language.

## Try it

LiveStylist's free trial gives you one full 5-minute session — no card required. Download the app and run a date night session before your next one.

Other use cases: Work · Casual · Weekend · All use cases · Home

---

# AI outfit advice for work and the office — LiveStylist
URL: https://livestylist.app/use-cases/work.html
LastModified: 2026-06-20

Use cases → Work

# AI outfit advice for work and the office

Work outfits are about repeatable confidence, not constant reinvention. The right pieces should feel obvious to put on, easy to combine, and quietly appropriate for whatever the day actually involves — a meeting, a presentation, a video call, or just sitting at your desk. LiveStylist's work sessions help you build that base and refine it over time, so the morning decision shrinks from twenty minutes to two.

## What the AI looks at

Fit and proportion on the top half — what reads sharp vs. fussy on a shirt, blouse, blazer or knit

Colour pairings that work across multiple outfits (a small reliable palette beats a closet full of one-offs)

How formal the look reads at a glance — meeting-ready vs. desk day

Whether the layers work together (blazer over shirt, sweater over collar) and what to take off

Tidy details: hair, jewellery, watch, glasses — the small things that add or subtract polish

## Example tips you might hear

The AI talks like a friend who knows their stuff — direct, specific, not preachy. A few illustrative lines from sessions:

"That collar is sitting a bit high. Pop one more button — opens up the neckline, reads more relaxed."

"The brown jacket fights with the cool tone of the shirt. Try a navy or charcoal layer instead."

"That blazer is reading slightly oversized on you. Roll the sleeves a quarter-turn, sit it back on the shoulder."

"For the presentation, skip the cardigan. Single-layer reads more confident on camera."

## How a session works for work

Open the LiveStylist app, pick Work from the occasion picker, and tap Start. You have five minutes. The AI opens with a quick read on what you're wearing, then asks if there's anything specific you want to focus on (a piece you're unsure about, the venue, the vibe). From there it's a conversation — you adjust, it re-evaluates, you ask follow-ups. Preview images of suggested looks render alongside the voice. At minute five the session wraps with a short summary you can save to your style memory.

## Common questions

Is it useful if my workplace is casual?

Yes. Tell the AI the dress-code at the start of the session — "smart casual office", "hybrid team, no blazer needed", "client-facing tomorrow" — and the advice calibrates accordingly.

Can it help me build a small capsule wardrobe?

Over multiple sessions, yes. The AI remembers what you have and what's worked. After a few sessions it'll start suggesting versatile pieces that bridge what you already own.

Does it work for video calls specifically?

Yes — mention you're going on camera. The AI prioritises colour against your background, contrast at the face, and whether patterns will moire on the webcam.

## Try it

LiveStylist's free trial gives you one full 5-minute session — no card required. Download the app and run a work session before your next one.

Other use cases: Date Night · Casual · Weekend · All use cases · Home

---

# AI outfit advice for casual looks — LiveStylist
URL: https://livestylist.app/use-cases/casual.html
LastModified: 2026-06-20

Use cases → Casual

# AI outfit advice for casual looks

Casual is the hardest category to nail because it looks like there are no rules. There are. The good casual outfits are the ones where every piece is deliberate but nothing screams effort. LiveStylist's casual sessions help you find that line: comfortable, current, considered — but never overdone.

## What the AI looks at

Whether the look feels effortless or accidentally sloppy

Fit on the relaxed top pieces — "oversized" only works in specific proportions

Layering that has movement and intent — t-shirt under shirt, shirt under jacket, sweatshirt and overshirt

Hair and face — light makeup, undone-but-deliberate hair that matches the vibe

One "hero" piece per outfit so it doesn't read flat — usually a jacket, a colour or a cap

## Example tips you might hear

The AI talks like a friend who knows their stuff — direct, specific, not preachy. A few illustrative lines from sessions:

"Push the sleeves up on the overshirt — instantly less stiff."

"Lose the necklace today. The collar is doing enough."

"That jacket is doing all the work. Keep the base layer plain so it reads as the focal point."

"Cap or no cap? Cap. It pulls the look together — without it, the top half is too neutral."

## How a session works for casual

Open the LiveStylist app, pick Casual from the occasion picker, and tap Start. You have five minutes. The AI opens with a quick read on what you're wearing, then asks if there's anything specific you want to focus on (a piece you're unsure about, the venue, the vibe). From there it's a conversation — you adjust, it re-evaluates, you ask follow-ups. Preview images of suggested looks render alongside the voice. At minute five the session wraps with a short summary you can save to your style memory.

## Common questions

Can it help with athleisure?

Yes — mention what you're going for (errands, gym to coffee, walk in the park) and the AI calibrates the line between athletic and lazy. Crucial for that category.

What about colours? I always end up in all black.

Common pattern. The AI can suggest a single colour anchor (one mid-tone piece — olive, rust, ecru) that breaks the all-black habit without forcing a full palette overhaul.

I don't know what "effortless" means. Can it just tell me?

Yes. Ask: "is this trying too hard?". The AI answers honestly and tells you what to take off.

## Try it

LiveStylist's free trial gives you one full 5-minute session — no card required. Download the app and run a casual session before your next one.

Other use cases: Date Night · Work · Weekend · All use cases · Home

---

# AI outfit advice for the weekend — LiveStylist
URL: https://livestylist.app/use-cases/weekend.html
LastModified: 2026-06-20

Use cases → Weekend

# AI outfit advice for the weekend

The weekend covers a lot — Saturday brunch, the casual drinks turned into a long evening, errands that turn into bumping into someone you know, the actual planned night out. LiveStylist's weekend sessions help you dress for the version of the day you actually want to have, not just the one you have planned.

## What the AI looks at

How adaptable the top half is — does it survive a vibe shift from day to night?

Comfort vs. impression — both matter; the AI helps you weight them

Statement vs. neutral — when the moment calls for one and not the other

Hair and accessories that read "weekend" not "work leftover"

One bright thing — a colour, an earring, a hero piece — to lift the look out of beige territory

## Example tips you might hear

The AI talks like a friend who knows their stuff — direct, specific, not preachy. A few illustrative lines from sessions:

"That top reads brunch. If you might end up at drinks later, throw a blazer or overshirt on it — works in both."

"Sleeves up. The whole look loosens — and you stop looking like you came from somewhere formal."

"Take the necklace off, leave the earrings. Stacking both reads heavy for daytime."

"One bright thing. Pick one. The rest neutral. The bright thing is what makes it weekend."

## How a session works for weekend

Open the LiveStylist app, pick Weekend from the occasion picker, and tap Start. You have five minutes. The AI opens with a quick read on what you're wearing, then asks if there's anything specific you want to focus on (a piece you're unsure about, the venue, the vibe). From there it's a conversation — you adjust, it re-evaluates, you ask follow-ups. Preview images of suggested looks render alongside the voice. At minute five the session wraps with a short summary you can save to your style memory.

## Common questions

Does it help with going-out looks?

Yes — mention you're going out, name the venue type if you can (cocktail bar, club, restaurant), and the AI adjusts. Statement makeup, more dramatic colour, a sharper top layer that reads going-out instead of brunch.

What about layering for unpredictable weather?

Tell the AI the forecast. It'll help you build something with a removable layer that reads intentional, not afterthought-coat.

Can it help me get out the door faster?

Yes. A five-minute session beats twenty minutes of trial and error in the mirror — the AI tells you what to commit to and you go.

## Try it

LiveStylist's free trial gives you one full 5-minute session — no card required. Download the app and run a weekend session before your next one.

Other use cases: Date Night · Work · Casual · All use cases · Home

---

# First date outfit advice from a live AI stylist — LiveStylist
URL: https://livestylist.app/use-cases/first-date.html
LastModified: 2026-06-20

Use cases → First date

# First date outfit advice from a live AI stylist

First dates are a styling problem because they're an information problem. You don't know the venue's vibe, you don't know what the other person is going to wear, you don't know whether the place is candlelit or under fluorescent strip lights. The instinct is to overthink and either over-dress (looks performative) or under-dress (reads underwhelmed). A five-minute live AI styling session with LiveStylist solves the version of the problem you can actually solve: it sees what you're wearing, hears the context, and tells you whether the look is landing where you want it to.

## What the AI looks at on a first-date session

How polished the look reads at a glance — first-date confidence vs. trying-too-hard is mostly a question of where the line falls between "considered" and "fussy"

Fit on the top half — what the other person will see across a table for the first ninety minutes

Colour against your skin tone — what brings the face forward vs. what washes it out under whatever lighting

One statement, rest quiet — a single confident choice (bold lip, structured jacket, statement earrings, slick low pony) with the rest of the look turned down

Shoes and the venue — what's walkable, what's weather-survivable, what reads with the rest of the outfit

The small de-formal things — sleeves rolled or not, top button open or not, hair up or down

## Example tips you might hear

Concrete and friendly, not preachy. A few illustrative lines from real sessions:

"That jacket is doing a lot of the work already. Drop the necklace — you don't need it competing."

"The lip colour is sharp against the sweater. Bring the eye up to match it or take the lip down a notch."

"Half-tuck the front of the shirt. It softens the silhouette without losing the line."

"This reads slightly business. If you're going to a wine bar, swap the loafers for something with a little more attitude."

"You're going for 'effortless'. Take off the second ring. Effortless usually means one less of something."

## How a session works for a first date

Open the LiveStylist app, tap Date Night, and start. You have five minutes — the box is intentional. The first thirty seconds, the AI reads what you're wearing and asks two things: where are you going, and how visible do you want to be? Both inputs matter. "Coffee at 4pm" and "cocktails at 9pm" call for genuinely different outfits, and "I want them to remember me" and "I don't want to overdo it" are both honest answers that change the advice.

The middle three minutes are the real session — back-and-forth. You make adjustments, the AI re-evaluates. Preview images render alongside the conversation, so when the AI says "lose the cardigan, swap to the leather jacket", you see the difference rather than imagining it. If you push back ("the leather jacket feels too much"), the AI adapts and tries again. It's a conversation, not a verdict.

The final thirty seconds, the AI summarises the look you've landed on and saves it to style memory. Next first-date session, it remembers what worked.

## What it doesn't do

Pretend to know things it can't. It can't tell you what the other person will be wearing, it can't predict the venue's lighting, and it doesn't read your mood any better than you do. What it can do is tell you, plainly, whether the outfit on you right now reads the way you want it to — which is the version of the problem you can actually fix in fifteen minutes before you leave.

## What changes between sessions

Style memory is the quiet feature that makes repeat first-date sessions sharper. The AI remembers what you wore last time, what you said worked, what you ended up taking off at the door, and what colours or silhouettes you keep returning to. By the third or fourth first-date session, the AI is no longer starting from scratch — it knows your version of "polished but easy" and can build on it instead of re-deriving it. That accumulation is the closest the app gets to having a stylist who actually knows you.

## Common questions

What should I wear on a first date?

Something you'd feel comfortable being photographed in. One statement piece, everything else quiet. Fit beats trend.

Should a first-date outfit be sexy or safe?

Aim for honest. Wear a version of yourself — your better, slightly polished version, not a costume. The AI flags when something is reading try-hard.

What if I don't know the venue?

Tell the AI. It'll suggest something deliberately middle-of-the-road — readable as either casual or dressed-up depending on what shows up.

How long before the date should I do the session?

Twenty minutes. Long enough to change something if needed, short enough you don't second-guess yourself into a worse outfit.

## Try it

LiveStylist's free trial gives you one full 5-minute session — no card required. Download the app and run a first-date session before your next one. Or see what a live session actually looks like first.

Other use cases: Date night · Work · Casual · Weekend · All use cases · AI stylist vs human stylist · Home

# What a live AI styling session actually looks like | LiveStylist
URL: https://livestylist.app/live-session-walkthrough.html
LastModified: 2026-06-20

Home → Live session walkthrough

# What a live AI styling session actually looks like

The shortest way to explain LiveStylist is to show one. Below is a real five-minute live session, screen by screen — the moment the app opens, the first time the AI sees the user's look, the suggestions, the preview images, and the final outfit it lands on. If you've ever wondered what "live AI stylist" means concretely instead of as marketing copy, this is it.

## Watch the 60-second demo

The live video below is the same product, condensed. Sound is muted by default for autoplay — tap the speaker icon to hear the AI talk.

Your browser doesn't support embedded video. Download the demo. A live AI styling session, condensed.

## The session, screen by screen

Same flow as the video, walked through carefully. Read it like a transcript.

0:00 — The app opens. The AI greets you by name. Because LiveStylist uses an anonymous device ID instead of a login, there's no sign-in screen between you and a session.

0:08 — Hey Nicole. A familiar, casual opener. The AI takes the time to acknowledge you before getting to the work — this is a conversation, not a form to fill.

0:15 — Pick the vibe. Tell the AI what you're aiming for. Date night? Office? Weekend? The session is calibrated to the occasion from the first second.

0:25 — Start. Tap once. The live vision pipeline spins up. Three vision agents (eye / mouth / outfit) start analysing your front-facing camera simultaneously, and the voice agent takes over the conversation.

1:20 — Let me show you a preview. When the AI suggests a change, it doesn't just describe it — it generates a preview image alongside the voice. You see what it means.

1:55 — Soft brown liner. The advice is concrete: not "maybe try makeup", but "soft brown liner across the lash line". Specific enough to act on, gentle enough not to feel prescribed.

2:20 — Enhance the eyes. A new suggestion, framed as a reason rather than a rule: the AI explains why — because the warmth of your top picks up the colour of your iris.

2:45 — Result preview. Side-by-side: before and after the suggested change, rendered as a preview image. You make the call.

3:10 — A little sparkle. Small finishing detail: an earring suggestion to balance the upper half of the outfit. The AI is calibrating the noise level, not just the volume.

3:35 — I love the suggestion. You can agree with the AI or push back. Both are normal. The session is a conversation, and the AI revises in real time when you disagree.

4:00 — Fresh look. Halfway through, a stocktake: the AI restates where you've landed so far, makes sure you still agree, and asks what else to refine.

4:45 — Compliments and advice. The closing minute is direct, warm, and substantive. Not gushing — useful. Whatever genuinely works gets named; whatever doesn't is noted with a reason.

5:00 — The final look. Session ends. You get a one-screen summary you can save to style memory. Next session picks up here — the AI remembers what worked and refines its advice across sessions.

## What was happening under the hood

While the conversation felt like one continuous voice, the system was running three things at once. A live vision pipeline (multi-agent: eye, mouth, outfit) analysed the camera frames every few seconds, processed in memory and discarded — nothing was written to disk. A live voice agent (Google Gemini Live) handled the dialogue with roughly 300ms response latency, so it felt like a person you could interrupt. And a preview-image generator (Fal.ai, Gemini 2.5 Flash Image) rendered the suggested looks alongside the conversation so suggestions were visual, not just verbal.

That triple-track is what makes LiveStylist's sessions feel like a video call with a stylist rather than a chat with a bot. It's also what no other AI stylist app in 2026 is doing.

## How long it actually takes

Five minutes, every time, by design. We tried longer sessions in early builds — they were worse. The five-minute box forces the AI to be useful immediately and forces you to take the advice instead of debating it forever. The pricing reflects this: Premium gives you thirty 5-minute sessions a month, which is roughly one a day. Most people use far fewer.

## What it costs

One free 5-minute session at signup, no card. After that, Premium is €14.99/month or €119.99/year. The annual saves you roughly 33% and is paid as one App Store / Google Play transaction. Full pricing.

## Common questions

How long is a live styling session?

Five minutes. Time-boxed deliberately — long enough for real advice, short enough to fit into the moment you're actually getting ready.

Can I interrupt the AI mid-sentence?

Yes. The voice agent supports natural turn-taking. Cut it off, change direction, ask a follow-up — it adapts.

Do I have to look perfect to start?

No. The whole point is to start from wherever you are. Pyjamas, bedhead, mid-getting-ready — the AI doesn't judge and nothing is stored.

## Try it

The free trial gives you one full 5-minute live session — no card required. Make your own mind up. Download the app, or read how it handles specific occasions: Date night, Work, Casual, Weekend, First date.

Related: AI stylist vs human stylist · Privacy Policy · How it works

# Compare AI stylist apps — LiveStylist vs alternatives
URL: https://livestylist.app/comparison/
LastModified: 2026-06-20

Home → Compare

# Compare AI stylist apps

Most "AI stylist" apps look the same in a screenshot — different brand, similar promise. Under the hood, they differ on the things that actually matter: do they see you in real time, do they talk back, how long does a session take, what do they cost, where does your data go. These comparison pages take the differences seriously and answer them honestly, naming names instead of dancing around them.

LiveStylist's wedge is simple to describe and hard to copy: live spoken voice + live camera analysis, in one five-minute conversation. Most competitors are photo-upload or closet-import — you give them a picture, they give you a board. That's a different product. The comparisons below explain when LiveStylist is the better fit and when it isn't.

## Comparisons

AI stylist vs human stylist — cost, speed, honesty, when to use which. The most common question we get.

More side-by-sides arriving alongside the launch. If there's an app you want compared, email hello@livestylist.app and we'll add it.

← Back to home

---

# AI stylist vs human stylist — honest comparison | LiveStylist
URL: https://livestylist.app/comparison/ai-stylist-vs-human.html
LastModified: 2026-06-20

Compare → AI stylist vs human stylist

# AI stylist vs human stylist: an honest comparison

The question shows up every time someone tries an AI stylist app for the first time: is this actually as good as a real human stylist? The honest answer is that the two solve overlapping but different problems, and the right one depends on what you're trying to fix. Here's the side-by-side, with the trade-offs spelt out plainly — written by the team building LiveStylist, a live AI stylist app, so there's a side, but we'd rather you understand the trade-offs than guess wrong.

## The five-second version

A live AI stylist is the right choice when you need fast, frequent, low-stakes advice (what to wear to dinner, the office, a Tuesday). A human stylist is the right choice when you need a full wardrobe overhaul, an in-person fitting, or someone to walk a department store with you. Most people need the first kind weekly and the second kind once a year, if ever.

## Cost

A live AI stylist subscription runs around €15/month — LiveStylist Premium is €14.99/month or €119.99/year. That gives you up to thirty live styling sessions a calendar month, each one five minutes of focused real-time advice. A one-off session with a human personal shopper at a department store typically costs nothing to €100, but only if you spend on clothes during the appointment. An independent fashion stylist for an hour of pure consulting starts around €100–€200 and can easily climb to €400+ for a full wardrobe edit. For ongoing styling — what to wear to the gala, the wedding, the new role — the human relationship can run into thousands a year.

If you want frequent, casual advice without the per-session anxiety, the maths overwhelmingly favours AI. If you want one big intervention every few years, a human is still affordable.

A live session in progress — the AI stylist speaking outfit advice in real time.

## Speed

A LiveStylist session is five minutes from "open app" to "advice in your head". A human stylist is a calendar invitation, a travel plan, a fitting room, a polite negotiation. There's a real reason to want the second one — touching the fabric, walking the floor, talking through your life — but when the question is "do these earrings work with this neckline before I leave the house in fifteen minutes", the AI's latency is a feature, not a workaround.

## Honesty

This one cuts both ways. A human stylist with a few sessions of trust will tell you the unkind truth — that the jacket reads cheap, that the colour washes you out. They have skin in the game. An AI will say the unkind truth too, faster, with no concern for offending you, because it doesn't remember tomorrow whether you took the note well. The downside is that an AI can't read your face when you flinch, and won't soften the next line because you're clearly having a hard week. Humans are better at calibrating delivery; AI is better at being unflinching when you specifically asked for that.

LiveStylist's voice agent is tuned to be direct and friendly — no scores, no ratings, no leaderboard. Ask it what doesn't work and it tells you what to take off.

## Availability and privacy

An AI stylist is available at 11pm on a Sunday in your pyjamas with bedhead. A human stylist is not. That sounds flippant but it's actually the main reason people use AI styling at all — the moments you need style advice are usually the moments you can't book a calendar invitation for.

Privacy-wise, LiveStylist's posture is unusually strict: no email or login required, camera frames processed in memory and never written to disk, voice audio not stored, no biometric template created, profile and session data hosted on EU-sovereign infrastructure (Scaleway, France). Human stylists are subject to no such guarantee — your fitting-room conversation is whatever your stylist remembers and tells whoever they tell.

## The honest side-by-side

Live AI stylist (LiveStylist) Human stylist Cost€14.99/mo or €119.99/yr€100–€400 per session Speed5 minutes, on demandDays to weeks of scheduling Availability24/7, your phoneOffice hours, in person Sees you in real timeYes (live camera + voice)Yes (in person) Touches the fabricNoYes Reads body languageLimited (visual cues only)Yes Remembers youYes (style memory across sessions)Yes (if you stick with one) Embarrassment-freeYes (no human, no fitting room)No, especially first session Best forDaily decisions, frequent adviceWardrobe overhaul, big events PrivacyFrames in memory only, EU-sovereign storage, no biometric templateWhatever the human remembers

## Where AI stylists actually differ from each other

"AI stylist" is a marketing label hiding several distinct products. Whering and Acloset are virtual closets — you import or photograph what you own, and they suggest outfits from your existing wardrobe. Indyx is a closet-digitisation app with optional human stylist support. Style DNA does selfie-based colour-season analysis. Beauty AI, Aiuta, TryDrobe and FashionAdvice.ai are photo-upload tools — you give them a picture, they give you feedback. Lookastic is a look library you browse manually.

None of them speak to you. None of them watch you live. LiveStylist is the only AI styling app with live spoken voice + live camera analysis in one conversation — which is the closest you can get to a stylist who is physically next to you without there being a person physically next to you.

Mid-session: the user agrees with a suggested look and a preview image renders alongside the conversation.

## When to use which

Use a live AI stylist when the question is small, the timeline is short, and the answer needs to be honest right now. "Does this read smart-casual?" "Will this colour wash me out on camera?" "Am I trying too hard?" The whole point of a live AI stylist is that the cost of asking is near zero, so you ask more.

Use a human stylist when the question is structural — you want a new wardrobe, a different personal aesthetic, advice that draws on touching the fabric and walking the store. Use them when the stakes are emotional in a way an AI can't hold (wedding dress, career-defining first day). Use them when you specifically want a human to disagree with you across a coffee.

Most people benefit from both, in the same way most people benefit from both a GP and a specialist. The AI is the GP — fast, available, good for the 90% of questions that don't need a specialist. The human is the specialist — expensive, scheduled, deeply useful for the cases that warrant them.

## Common questions

Is an AI stylist actually as good as a human stylist?

For day-to-day outfit decisions, yes — and faster, cheaper, more available. For a full wardrobe overhaul, a special occasion or an in-person fitting, a human still wins. Most people need the first kind much more often than the second.

How much does an AI stylist cost compared to a human?

Roughly €15/month for LiveStylist Premium (€14.99/mo or €119.99/yr). One human stylist session is typically €100–€400. Ongoing human styling adds up fast; an AI subscription doesn't.

Will the AI judge me?

No. No scores, no ratings, no leaderboards. Camera frames are processed in memory and not stored — even the AI forgets the session unless you save the summary.

What about other AI stylist apps?

Whering, Acloset, Indyx, Style DNA, Beauty AI, Aiuta, TryDrobe, FashionAdvice.ai and Lookastic all solve adjacent problems (closet management, photo feedback, colour analysis). None are live-voice + live-camera. LiveStylist is.

## Try LiveStylist

The free trial gives you one full 5-minute live session — no card required. Make up your own mind. Download the app, or read more about how a live session actually works.

Related: About LiveStylist · Pricing · Use cases · All comparisons

---

# Privacy Policy | LiveStylist — AI Style Assistant
URL: https://livestylist.app/privacy.html
LastModified: 2026-06-19

# Privacy Policy

Last updated: June 19, 2026

## 1. Information We Collect

LiveStylist is built around a minimal data model. We do not require an email address or login. Your account is identified only by an anonymous device identifier (a randomly-generated UUID stored on your device).

Camera Data: While a session is active, your front-facing camera streams cropped frames (eye, mouth, body) to our AI vision pipeline. Frames are processed in memory and are not persisted to disk on our servers.

Voice Data: Microphone audio is streamed in real time to Google's Gemini Live API for transcription and dialogue. We do not store the raw audio. Transcripts are kept only as part of your session memory (see below).

Profile Data: The name, favorite color, optional stylist nickname, and language preference you provide during onboarding.

Session Data: Start time, end time, duration, and a short text summary of each session ("session memory") so the stylist can reference past sessions.

Generated Images: Style preview images generated during sessions are temporarily hosted on Fal.ai's content delivery network. We do not retain copies on our servers.

Subscription Data: If you purchase Premium, an anonymous subscriber record is kept by RevenueCat (which integrates with Apple App Store and Google Play). We do not see your payment details.

We do not collect email addresses, real names, contacts, location, or device contact lists. We do not use third-party analytics or advertising trackers.

## 2. Face Data

Because LiveStylist's camera frames include the user's face, we treat face imagery as a distinct, sensitive category and disclose it explicitly here.

What face data is collected: During an active styling session, the app captures short-lived cropped frames from your front-facing camera covering your face — specifically the eye region, the mouth region, and the upper-body / outfit region around a face-guide oval shown on screen. No raw full-frame photos or videos are saved.

All planned uses of face data: real-time style and beauty analysis only — i.e. commentary on hair, makeup, eye/lip color choices, accessories, and upper-body outfit. Face data is not used for identification, biometric matching, attractiveness scoring, advertising, profile building, or training any AI model.

Who face data is shared with, and where it is stored: the cropped frames are sent over a TLS-encrypted connection to our backend, which forwards them to Google (Gemini API) for in-memory computer-vision inference. Google processes them under its Enterprise Privacy Commitment. Face frames are not persisted to our database, are not sold, and are not shared with advertisers. Generated style-preview images (which may include your likeness) are returned via Fal.ai's CDN and served for a short window only.

Retention of face data: camera frames containing your face are processed in memory and are never written to disk on our servers. Nothing facial is retained after the session ends. The only post-session artifact is a short text summary of the styling advice ("session memory"), which does not contain face data.

No biometric identifier: LiveStylist does not create, derive, or store a biometric template, face embedding, or any identifier that could be used to recognise you across sessions or apps. Apple's on-device Face ID is not used by LiveStylist.

If you want this data removed, simply end the session — face frames disappear from memory immediately. Deleting your account (Profile → Delete Account) additionally removes all stored profile data and session summaries.

## 3. How We Use Your Information

To provide real-time AI-powered style and beauty advice during a session.

To remember your preferences and recent sessions so the stylist can be personal across visits.

To enforce subscription limits (free trial vs. Premium).

To diagnose and fix bugs.

## 4. Data Sharing & Sub-processors

We do not sell your personal data. To run the service, the following sub-processors handle data on our behalf:

Google (Gemini API): Real-time voice conversation and computer-vision analysis of camera frames. Data is processed under Google's Enterprise Privacy Commitment.

Fal.ai: AI image generation for style previews (model: Gemini 2.5 Flash Image, hosted via Fal). Source images and prompts are sent to Fal for inference; generated images are returned via Fal's CDN.

Eurobase (Scaleway, France): Our application database. All your profile and session data is stored on EU infrastructure (France) and never leaves the EU.

Google Cloud Run (US): Hosts our backend API and orchestrates the calls above. The backend is stateless — it forwards data; it does not store your session content beyond what's persisted to Eurobase.

RevenueCat: Subscription management and entitlement checking (only if you purchase Premium). Receives an anonymous subscriber identifier.

Apple App Store / Google Play Store: Payment processing for subscriptions. We never see your payment details.

## 5. Data Retention

Camera frames: processed in memory only; not stored.

Audio: processed in real time by Google Gemini Live; not stored by us.

Session memories & profile: retained for as long as your account exists.

Generated preview images: served from Fal.ai's CDN for a short window (typically up to 24 hours) and not retained by us.

You can delete your entire account from inside the app (Profile → Delete Account). This permanently removes your profile, all session records, and all session memories from our servers.

## 6. Your Rights (GDPR & similar)

Because we collect almost no personal data, most rights are fulfilled automatically: there is no email or real name to access, correct, or export. You always have the right to:

Delete your account and all associated data (Profile → Delete Account).

Stop using the app and uninstall it (the local device ID is destroyed).

Cancel your subscription at any time via your device's subscription settings.

## 7. Data Security

All network traffic is encrypted in transit using TLS. Data at rest in Eurobase is encrypted by the underlying database. API keys for sub-processors are stored in Google Secret Manager and are never embedded in the app.

## 8. Children's Privacy

LiveStylist is not intended for users under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect data from children under this age.

## 9. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of significant changes via the app or by updating the "Last updated" date above.

## 10. Contact Us

If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, contact us at:

privacy@livestylist.app

---

# Terms & Conditions | LiveStylist — AI Style Assistant
URL: https://livestylist.app/terms.html
LastModified: 2026-05-11

# Terms & Conditions

Last updated: May 11, 2026

## 1. Acceptance of Terms

By downloading, installing, or using LiveStylist ("the App"), you agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions. If you do not agree, do not use the App.

## 2. Description of Service

LiveStylist is an AI-powered personal styling assistant that provides real-time beauty and fashion advice through computer vision and voice interaction. The service is provided "as is" and advice given by the AI is for informational and entertainment purposes only.

## 3. User Accounts

You may be required to create an account to use certain features. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities under your account.

## 4. Acceptable Use

You agree not to:

Use the App for any unlawful purpose

Attempt to reverse-engineer, decompile, or extract the AI models

Use the camera feature to capture or process images of individuals without their consent

Share, redistribute, or commercially exploit content generated by the App

Interfere with or disrupt the service or its infrastructure

Create multiple accounts to circumvent usage limits

## 5. Subscriptions & Payments

Premium subscriptions are billed monthly or annually through the Apple App Store or Google Play Store. Subscription terms, cancellation policies, and refund procedures are subject to the respective store's policies.

Free Trial: 1 lifetime trial session at signup, 5 minutes long

Premium Monthly: $14.99 / month — daily styling sessions, 5 minutes per session, soft cap of 30 sessions per calendar month

Premium Annual: $119.99 / year — same as monthly, ~33% discount

## 6. AI-Generated Content

Style advice provided by LiveStylist is generated by artificial intelligence and should not be considered professional fashion consulting. The AI may occasionally provide inaccurate or inappropriate suggestions. LiveStylist Ltd. is not liable for decisions made based on AI-generated advice.

## 7. Intellectual Property

All content, features, and functionality of the App — including but not limited to text, graphics, logos, AI models, and software — are owned by LiveStylist Ltd. and protected by intellectual property laws.

## 8. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, LiveStylist Ltd. shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the App.

## 9. Termination

We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account at any time for violation of these Terms, without prior notice. Upon termination, your right to use the App will immediately cease.

## 10. Changes to Terms

We may update these Terms from time to time. Continued use of the App after changes constitutes acceptance of the new Terms.

## 11. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of England and Wales.

## 12. Contact

For questions regarding these Terms, contact us at:

legal@livestylist.app LiveStylist Ltd., 123 Style Street, London EC1A 1AA, United Kingdom

---

# Community Guidelines | LiveStylist — AI Style Assistant
URL: https://livestylist.app/community-guidelines.html
LastModified: 2026-05-18

# Community Guidelines

Last updated: May 18, 2026

LiveStylist is a place to get real-time style advice from an AI stylist, and to share style sessions with friends you've connected with. Everyone is welcome — and we keep it that way by holding the community to a few simple rules.

## What's allowed

Sessions about clothing, makeup, accessories, hair, and personal style.

Sharing your finished sessions with friends you've followed back.

Honest feedback and questions about your own style.

## What's not allowed

Nudity or sexual content. LiveStylist is not a place for explicit material. Keep yourself and others clothed in front of the camera.

Violence or threats. No threats, no content depicting harm against people or animals.

Harassment or hate speech. No targeting individuals or groups based on identity, appearance, or any protected characteristic.

Impersonation. Don't pretend to be someone else. Don't use someone else's name, image, or magic ID without their consent.

Spam, scams, and self-promotion. Don't use the app or your sessions to push unrelated products, links, or fake offers.

Illegal activity. Anything that breaks the law in your jurisdiction.

## How to report

If you see something that breaks these rules:

A session in your feed: open the session, tap the ⋯ in the top right, pick a category, optionally add a note, and submit.

A follow request: on the Friends screen, hold (long-press) the requester's name and tap to report. The request is automatically declined.

Reports go directly to a human reviewer. You'll stop seeing the reported content immediately. If we agree it breaks the guidelines, we remove it globally (and may suspend the account that posted it).

## How we moderate

Every report is reviewed within 24 hours.

We may remove individual sessions, hide a user's content from feeds, or suspend an account, depending on severity.

Repeat or serious violations result in a permanent ban.

We will not share who reported what with the person being reported.

## Blocking

You can block any user from your follow list. Blocking removes them from your feed, prevents future follow requests, and breaks any existing follow relationship — without notifying them. Blocked users cannot see your sessions.

## Appeals

If we removed your content or restricted your account and you believe we got it wrong, email appeals@livestylist.app. Include the date, what was removed, and why you think it was a mistake. We respond within 7 days.

## Changes to these guidelines

We may update these guidelines as the community grows or new edge cases arise. The "Last updated" date at the top reflects the most recent change. Material changes will be communicated in-app on the next launch.

## Contact

For questions about these guidelines (not appeals): support@livestylist.app

---

Stefan Gimeson 123 Style Street, London EC1A 1AA, United Kingdom

## Dispute Resolution

The European Commission provides a platform for online dispute resolution (OS): https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr. We are not willing or obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

## Liability for Content

As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages according to general laws. However, we are not obligated to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information. Obligations to remove or block the use of information under general law remain unaffected.

## Liability for Links

Our website may contain links to external third-party websites over whose content we have no influence. We cannot accept any liability for this external content. The respective provider or operator is always responsible for the content of the linked pages.
